title: feat(personhog): route methods to different grpc channels
body:
## Problem

Lightweight queries in personhog are showing some fat tail latencies. The problem is possibly due to TCP-level head-of-line blocking: there are 4 HTTP/2 connections from each router pod to the replicas, and each connection carries all the RPCs. Larger `GetGroupsBatch` responses cause TCP congestion that stalls small responses sharing the same connections.

## Changes

Split the router's single gRPC channel pool into two isolated pools, so small responses never share a TCP conne

title: fix(replay-vision): flush kafka producer before checking side-effect result

## Problem

`emit_classifier_tags_activity` and `embed_indexer_observation_activity` (from #59388) await `ProduceResult.get(timeout=10)`. Nothing else polls the cached producer, so the delivery callback never fires and `.get()` always times out — confirmed in prod, every observation of those two scanner types fails.

## Changes

Wrap each activity's produce in `producer_scope(..., flush_timeout=10)` so the shared producer is flushed (and the callback fires) before `.get(timeout=0)` surfaces brok

title: chore(personhog): metric improvements
body:
## Problem

The personhog service Grafana dashboard computes latency decomposition by subtracting independent histogram quantiles — e.g., `p99(router_backend) - p99(replica_grpc)` for the "Network / gRPC transport overhead" panel.
This is mathematically imprecise: `p99(A) - p99(B) ≠ p99(A - B)` because different requests can dominate the tails of each histogram independently.
For the cross-service panel (router → replica), this overstates or misstates the actual transport overhead at extreme

title: fix(property-vals): spread Kafka produces across partitions by widening the key
body: ## Problem The producer used \`team_id.to_string()\` as the Kafka message key. Kafka hashes the key pick a partition, so every message from a single team landed on the same partition. The CH Kafka engine assigns one consumer thread per partition, so a high-volume team bottlenecks the entire pipeline through that one thread. Observed in prod-us at 10% rollout: partition 18 held 78% of total output lag (32M out of 41M) while 63 of 64 partitions were nearly idle.

title: feat(api): advertise OAuth resource metadata on 401 responses
body:
## Problem

Agents (MCP-style clients, in particular) that hit our API with no
credentials need a way to bootstrap OAuth discovery from a stock 401
response. RFC 9728 standardizes this: the server returns
`WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="<URL>"`, and the client
fetches the protected-resource metadata document from that URL.

Today our DRF 401s carry no `WWW-Authenticate` header, so agents have
to be configured out-of-band to know where the discovery document
lives.

## Changes

- `po

- **Signing email identity** (`sender`) — the "From" address recipients see when the signing envelope is dispatched

The capture HTTP metrics middleware emits a `path` label on `http_requests_total`, `http_requests_duration_seconds`, `middleware_pass_total`, and `middleware_request_timed_out_total`. For requests that don't match a registered route, the middleware fell back to `normalize_unmatched_path`, which keeps the first path segment as the label value. That still produces one new label per distinct first segment (scanner probes, typos, arbitrary client paths), inflating series cardinality and

This also adds session id initialization so we can use it for analytics, this is allowed as per the spec even though the server is stateless

The inputs had no `autoComplete` attribute, so browsers heuristically treated them as login fields.

The hog-charts library is the new in-house chart toolkit gradually replacing Chart.js for trends visualizations. The legacy BoxPlot trend uses Chart.js via the `chartjs-chart-boxplot` plugin. To migrate it, we need a native hog-charts BoxPlot primitive that follows the library's layering conventions (pure geometry + canvas drawing primitives + hit-testing + React component) and reuses the BarChart band/group scale infrastructure.

This PR only adds the **library primitive**. Wiri

title: fix(clickhouse): handle missing posthog_instancesetting table during migrations

When Postgres and ClickHouse migrations run in parallel (the default in bin/migrate), ClickHouse migration 0019 calls sync_execute which hits get_kill_switch_level -> InstanceSetting.objects.filter(...) before the posthog_instancesetting table has been created, causing a ProgrammingError.

## Changes

Wrap the instance-setting reads in _get_kill_switch_level and _get_hedged_app_queries_enabled with broad exception handling so they fall back to safe defaults (OFF / False) when t

body:
## Problem

Experiment timeseries precomputation only runs for experiments whose `start_date` is within the last 30 days. Experiments running longer than that get no automatic recalculation.

## Changes

Bumped the cutoff from 30 to 60 days. Introduced `EXPERIMENT_RECALCULATION_MAX_AGE_DAYS` constant in `posthog/temporal/experiments/activities.py` (used by both the regular and saved-metric activity filters) and a mirrored constant in `frontend/src/scenes/experiments/constants.ts` used by the sta

title: fix(ingestion): sanitize null bytes from group_key before postgres query
body:
## Problem

Events with null bytes (`\x00`) in `$group_key` cause PostgreSQL to reject the query with `invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0x00`. This surfaces as a query error on the `fetchGroup` SELECT before the upsert ever runs.

The CDP path (`groups-manager.service.ts`) already sanitized group keys via `sanitizeString`, but the ingestion pipeline path was missing the same guard.

## Changes

- Apply `sanitizeString` to `$group_key` in `upsertGroup` before passing it to the group sto

The CLI tool was rolled out to everyone, so let's clean the flag.

## Changes

- Drop the `mcp-single-exec-tool` flag lookup from both code paths that resolve mode/version: the Hono `RequestStateResolver` and the legacy `MCP` durable object's `init()`.
- Simplify `resolveModeAndVersion` so single-exec activates purely on the client-profile heuristic (or an explicit `mode=cli`); `mode=tools` still wins as a kill switch.
- Remove the now-unused `resolveSingleExecFlag` helper an

title: fix(mcp_store): forward x-posthog-* headers through the store proxy
body:
## Problem

PostHog Code tasks using a Store-installed PostHog MCP hit `Tool exec not found` on every `exec` call. The Store proxy drops `x-posthog-mcp-consumer`, so `useSingleExec` resolves false upstream. The built-in config path (`get_sandbox_ph_mcp_configs`) was already setting the header.

## Changes

- Proxy forwards `x-posthog-mcp-*` from the incoming request. Non-PostHog upstreams ignore unknown headers in that namespace.
- `get_user_mcp_server_configs` now sets the consumer from `intera

title: fix(tasks): stop stamping hedgehog reaction on interim slack relays
body:
## Problem

When a Slack-launched task runs, the bot manages a reaction on the user's mention message as a status indicator:

| Reaction | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `:seedling:` | task received / queued |
| `:eyes:` | agent working |
| `:hedgehog:` | task completed (or PR opened) |
| `:x:` | task failed |

`post_slack_update` is the intended single source of truth for terminal reactions — it only stamps `:hedgehog:` when the run reaches `COMPLETED`, `CANCELLED`, or a PR is opened.

However, ever

Trends pie/donut insights still render via Chart.js, while line and bar have moved to PostHog's canvas-based `hog-charts` library behind the `PRODUCT_ANALYTICS_HOG_CHARTS_TRENDS` flag. Pie/donut is the last common display type that hasn't migrated, so the Chart.js bundle stays in the eager dashboard build and the rendering pipeline stays split across two libraries.

Lifecycle is the last of the time-series trends views still rendering through the legacy Chart.js `LineGraph`. Trends and stickiness already have their hog-charts ports gated behind feature flags; lifecycle has been explicitly excluded because it needs negative stacking (the `dormant` series sits below the zero baseline) which the bar chart didn't support.

Stickiness insights still render through the Chart.js `ActionsLineGraph` because `Trends.tsx` explicitly excludes them (`!isStickiness`) from the hog-charts line branch. To finish the hog-charts migration we need a stickiness-specific port that preserves the current behavior — especially the percent y-axis, interval-count x-axis, and the `<label> stickiness on <interval> <day>` modal title — without changing anything user-visible until we flip the flag.

Adds `PRODUCT_ANA

In read-only mode, the toast that fires on a blocked write tells users to "Use Max or the MCP to make this change" — but POSTs to `/api/environments/:team_id/conversations` were themselves being blocked by `assertNotReadOnly`. Starting a new PostHog AI (Max) conversation while read-only is on failed with the same toast that just instructed the user to talk to Max. The escape hatch and the trap were the same path.

Allowlist the AI conversations URL space in `frontend/src/

body:
## Problem

The lazy precompute path (PR #59075) currently has one Prometheus metric: `web_overview_lazy_precompute_failed_total` (and the stats equivalent). Everything else about lazy adoption — gate rejections, eligible-but-cache-miss fall-throughs, successful serves — is logged but not counted.

That makes it impossible to answer rollout-health questions like "why isn't team X seeing lazy?" or "what % of eligible requests are still falling back to the raw path?" from Grafana without log divin

title: feat(web-analytics): lazy precomputation for web vitals paths tile
body:
## Problem

The Web Vitals path-breakdown tile (Web Analytics → Web Vitals tab) scans every `$web_vitals` event in the date range and runs a `quantile(p)` per `$pathname` on every dashboard load. That cost is paid by every visitor regardless of whether the data changed since the last load.

This PR extends the existing lazy precomputation framework (already powering the Web Overview tile and the Web Stats table) to cover the path-breakdown tile, gated behind the same `web-analytics-precompute-to

title: fix(insights): allow viewed/timing/metalytics telemetry POSTs through read-only guard
body:
## Problem

Closes a fresh `ReadOnlyModeError: You are in read-only mode` from error tracking, originally fired at `insightUsageLogic.ts:68` → `api.create` → `assertNotReadOnly` in `readOnlyGuard.ts`.

Self read-only mode (#59372) gates writes through `assertNotReadOnly`, which throws on any `PATCH | PUT | POST | DELETE` whose URL is not on a small allowlist. Several passive client-side telemetry POSTs fire automatically on view/mount and aren't user-initiated writes — but the guard was treating

`/api/llm_analytics/@me/spend/` was 500'ing in production with:

```
DB::Exception: Identifier '__table5.pmat_email' cannot be resolved

The endpoint queries `ai_events` (the satellite ClickHouse cluster optimized for AI observability) joined to `person.properties.email`. The HogQL printer rewrites `person.properties.email` → `<person_table>.pmat_email` whenever the materialized column is registered on `person`. On the main cluster that's fine — the column exists. On the satellit

The `PRODUCT_ANALYTICS_HOG_CHARTS*` feature flags fragmented across five distinct flags — one per insight type, plus an extra split inside trends (line vs bar) and inside retention (line vs bar). | `PRODUCT_ANALYTICS_HOG_CHARTS` (trends li

The flag-gated hog-charts retention visualizations live under `frontend/src/scenes/retention/viz/`, but the rest of the trends/retention chart code has already moved (or will move) under `products/product_analytics/frontend/insights/`. title: ci: skip docker image build when unused, harden e2e changes checkout
body:
## Summary

Two unrelated CI fixes I noticed while debugging slow CI on a 1-line test PR ([#59779](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/59779)). Both touch only `.github/workflows/`, both are easy to revert independently.

### 1. Skip `Build Docker image` when no consumer waits for it

`container-images-ci.yml`'s `posthog_build` runs ~15–25 min on `depot-ubuntu-latest-4` and is gated by:

```yaml
docker_files:
  - '**'
  - '!rust/**'
  - '!livestream/**'
```

i.e. *anything except rust/livest

`frontend/src/lib/components/ReverseProxyChecker/reverseProxyCheckerLogic.test.ts` has a single failing test on master (`should not report read-only mode blocks to error tracking`, added by [#59645](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/59645)). I noticed it while wrapping up CI on an unrelated PR ([#59518](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/59518)); the failure reproduces on a clean `origin/master` worktree using a fresh `pnpm install` + the same `jest` invocation, so it's a

title: refactor(integrations): Extract github_callback
body:
## Problem

`posthog/api/integration.py` and `posthog/api/user_integration.py` accumulated GitHub OAuth/install callback cruft: two different cache key schemas, web vs. desktop redirects, the `github_link_complete` view body, and three viewset actions that duplicated some installation-ID validation and ownership checks.

The next step of the GitHub integration robustness work needs to introduce a bunch of functionality improvements, but doing that on top of the existing layout would be a mess, m

title: feat(mcp): rate limiting
body:
## Problem

The MCP server has no per-token request throttling today, so a misbehaving agent (or a tight retry loop) can drive arbitrary load through `/mcp`. We want a basic safety net: cap a single token's request rate without getting in the way of legitimate agent workflows.

## Changes

Per-token rate limiting on the Hono `/mcp` endpoint, backed by Redis `INCR` + `EXPIRE` for a fixed-window counter. Two windows are checked on every request, matching PostHog's REST API default throttle in `pos

title: chore(mcp): broaden integration test coverage
body:
## Problem

The MCP server's integration suite already wires the official `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` client against the real Hono listener and the real PostHog stack, but the breadth of cases it covers is narrow — initialize, list, call, plus a handful of resilience tests. A green run did not guarantee that public routes, auth enforcement, JSON-RPC edge cases, session negotiation, or real upstream tool calls actually work.

This PR widens the suite so the assertion "if it's green, the MCP serve

title: chore(mcp): reuse master schema cache to skip full migrations
body:
## Problem

MCP integration tests were running `python manage.py migrate` from scratch on every PR run, which dominated the setup time of the `Integration Tests` job. Backend pytest jobs in `ci-backend.yml` already solved this — they restore a pre-built `schema.sql.gz` produced by the master-only `check-migrations` job and pass `--reuse-db` to pytest so Django only applies the delta added by the PR. The MCP workflow wasn't plugged into that cache, so it paid the full migration cost.

## Changes

title: feat(taxonomic-filter): batch suggested search results behind a reveal barrier
body:
## Problem

The Suggested filters tab fired every per-group query in parallel, then revealed each row as its query resolved. Slower groups returning empty pushed earlier rows around as the list reshuffled, and a group with no results above a heavy group could yank the user's hover target out from under them mid-click.

It also showed the same underlying item twice — once as a Recent row (e.g. "Events · pageview") and again as the Events group's own row for the same value — because Recents are co

title: fix(taxonomic-filter): preserve identifier fields on pinned items
body:
## Problem

A new error tracking issue surfaced: hovering a pinned **Person** item in the `TaxonomicFilter` crashed the popover with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')`.

Root cause: when an item is pinned, `taxonomicFilterPinnedPropertiesLogic` shrank it to `{ name }` before persisting to localStorage. But several source groups' `getValue` read other fields:

- `Persons` -> `person.distinct_ids[0]`
- `Insights` -> `insight.short_id`
- `Actions` -> `action.id`
- `Noteb

title: feat(ux): remove posthog internal scene tabs
body:
## Problem

PostHog's internal scene tabs (browser-like tab strip above the scene area) added complexity without clear user value. We're removing them in favor of native browser tabs.

### Usage from PostHog MCP (PostHog App + Website, project 2)

| Event | Last 7 days (events / users) | Last 30 days (events / users) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `tab opened` | 91,394 / 22,264 | 373,621 / 59,486 |
| `tab closed` | 33,772 / 9,328 | 137,953 / 27,640 |
| `$autocapture` on `scene-tab-new-button`

title: fix(experiments): implement deduplication backfill as a management command.
body:
## Problem

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

PR #59729 fixed the live path so new writes can't introduce duplicate metric UUIDs, but pre-existing corrupt rows still carry duplicates from MCP/LLM update flows that copied metrics without regenerating their UUIDs. The runtime no longer blocks delete/update on those rows (the validator only inspects the incoming payload), but the corruption is still in the DB, and any future feature that assumes UUI

## Problem

Firefox users hitting a stale PostHog deploy see a hard error instead of being auto-reloaded by `ChunkLoadErrorBoundary`. The boundary's reload-once safety net only fires when `isChunkLoadError` recognizes the error shape, and Firefox emits two different messages for failed dynamic imports: `NetworkError when attempting to fetch resource.` (already handled) and `error loading dynamically imported module: <url>` (not handled). The latter is what Firefox raises when a deferred `import(

title: fix(llma): extract pydantic-ai tool calls from output parts
body:
## Problem

Pydantic AI's current OpenTelemetry instrumentation stores generation output in `gen_ai.output.messages`, which PostHog maps to `$ai_output_choices`. Tool calls in that output are represented as message `parts` with `type: "tool_call"` and a top-level `name`.

PostHog already extracts tool calls from several provider shapes, but not this Pydantic AI message-parts shape, so affected `$ai_generation` events do not get `$ai_tools_called` or `$ai_tool_call_count` populated.

Pydantic AI

title: chore(feature-flags): add finding-deleted-feature-flags skill
body:
## Problem

When someone asks an agent \"what feature flags got deleted in the last week?\", the agent can't one-shot the answer. `system.feature_flags` exposes the soft-delete `deleted` boolean but **no deletion timestamp** (`deleted_at` / `updated_at` / `last_modified_at` are all unresolvable). So a single SQL query can't filter by deletion time — the actual deleted-at lives only in the per-flag activity log, reached via `feature-flags-activity-retrieve` (one call per flag, no bulk endpoint).

title: fix(notebooks): treat client-ahead conflicts as stale instead of empty-step loops
body:
## Problem

When the Redis stream's latest version is below the client's `last_seen_version`, the server reports a 409 conflict but `_fetch_missed_steps` asks Redis for an inverted XRANGE — gets nothing back — and the trim-detection guard (`len(missed) < gap_size`) silently passes because `gap_size` is negative. So we return 409 with an empty `steps` array. The client has nothing to rebase, retries with the same baseline, gets the same 409 — infinite loop.

title: fix(hogql): reject statement-level SAMPLE, attach pivot-table sample
body:
## Problem

We were silently discard SAMPLE clauses in the C++ parser, for specific grammar constructions. This PR makes those constructions throw instead of silently discarding.

Claude continues:

The HogQL grammar's `selectStmt` rule admits a statement-level `(USING? sampleClause)?` in two positions (`HogQLParser.g4:75` and `:79`). This is DuckDB's `USING SAMPLE` syntax (added with the duck/postgres surface in #50353), which is semantically distinct from ClickHouse's table-level `SAMPLE

## Problem

The MCP analytics **sessions** list was backed by a Temporal job (`backfill-mcp-sessions`) that re-aggregated every active session's full history across **all teams** every 5 minutes into a Postgres `MCPSession` mirror. That job was disabled because its cost scaled with total event volume and cadence rather than with what anyone actually viewed: it couldn't use the events sort key (no `team_id` in the query) and did per-row Postgres upserts. With it off, the sessions dashboard reads

title: fix(cdp): shrink the hog invocation results payload
body:
## Problem

The `hog_invocation_results` lifecycle row (powering the CDP runs UI + rerun path) stores the invocation's globals tree in the `invocation_globals` column, produced as plain JSON to the warpstream-cyclotron Kafka cluster. Profiling production traffic showed this one field is ~99% of every row's bytes (~64 KiB/row) and the dominant driver of cyclotron write throughput — far more than the "one extra event per event" the design assumed. A per-key breakdown: `groups` ~52%, `event` ~37%,

title: fix(data-warehouse): recover postgres imports from dropped connections
body:
## Problem

Long-running Data warehouse Postgres imports were failing with `ProtocolViolation: server conn crashed?` even on syncs that had been progressing for hours.

Root cause: the streaming read path holds a server-side cursor open for the whole sync, and a named cursor in Postgres only exists inside an open transaction. `get_rows` is a generator, so every `yield` suspends the producer while the consumer performs the delta-merge — which can take minutes. During that time the source backend

## Problem

  1. **No retry backoff.** A batch landing in `waiting_retry` was eligible to be re-picked on the very next poll cycle, so transient failures (network blips, downstream pressure) could be retried tightly until
  `max_attempts` was burned through.
  2. **No recovery grace window.** The orphan sweep treated any `executing` row whose advisory lock wasn't held as stale, including rows that had only existed for milliseconds. A worker that briefly dropped its  psycopg session  or one w

The 2FA reset email triggered from Django admin tells users the reset link will expire in **1 hour**, but the underlying token is actually valid for **24 hours**. This mismatch is misleading and could cause users to abandon a perfectly valid reset link.

The `expiration_hours` value passed to the email template in `posthog/tasks/email.py` was hardcoded to `1`, while the real timeout is enforced in `posthog/api/two_factor_reset.py` using the literal `86400` seconds, repeated in three

- The **Cymbal capture pipeline** (unauthenticated, public Project API Key) writes rows derived from JS frames carrying both a `chunk_id` and a `filename` URL. Until now, `OrChunkId::Both`'s `Display` impl collapsed the ref to the chunk id, so captu

title: perf(hogql): rust-py backend - direct PyO3 ast construction
body:
## Problem

The freshly-merged Rust HogQL parser (#58949) ships with one PyO3 entry point shape: `parse_*_json` returns a JSON-serialised AST string that Python then deserialises into `posthog.hogql.ast` dataclasses (via `json_ast.py::_deserialize_node`). Both sides pay for the round-trip — Rust serialises a `serde_json::Value` into a string, then CPython rebuilds the same shape into typed dataclass instances. On heavy queries the JSON round-trip is a measurable fraction of total parse time.

Th

title: fix(personhog): drop http scheme from gRPC target address
body:
## Problem

`PERSONHOG_ADDR` feeds `grpc.insecure_channel`, which expects a bare `host:port` (or a `dns:///` URI). The `http://` prefix caused gRPC's DNS resolver to try resolving the literal string `http://127.0.0.1` as a hostname, so every personhog RPC terminated with `StatusCode.UNAVAILABLE`. This silently broke person and group lookups in the local dev environment and the Docker Hobby deploy.

## Changes

- `.env.development`: `PERSONHOG_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:50052` becomes `127.0.0.1:50052

The per-rule 24h drop counts on the drop-rules settings table only refreshed when the section component re-mounted (`afterMount` → `loadRules` → `loadRulesSuccess` → `loadRuleDropImpact`). A user who landed on the settings page, enabled or edited a rule, and stayed on the page could watch the impact column stay stuck on the pre-change number indefinitely — until they hit the manual "Refresh list" button or navigated away and back.

This was especially confusing alongside the rule det

title: feat(flags): loosen flags cache verifier and pass through unknown keys
body:
## Problem

The flags cache verifier compares Python (DB source of truth) against the Rust warm-flags-cache output and reports field divergences. Two patterns produce spurious mismatches that aren't real divergence.

First, absent vs explicit null. Python sometimes serializes a missing field as omitted, Rust sometimes serializes it as `null`. The matcher treats these identically, but the verifier flags them as different. This affects roughly 66k flags.

Second, unknown JSONB keys. Rust deseriali

title: fix(logs): deterministic drops outrank rate_limit regardless of priority
body:
## Problem

A record that any deterministic drop rule (`path_drop` or `severity_sampling`) would resolve was being charged against a `rate_limit` rule's token bucket whenever the rate-limit rule sat earlier in the priority-ordered rule list. That was wasted bucket capacity — the record was always going to be dropped (or explicitly kept) — and it starved legitimate traffic for no reason.

The previous policy let _any_ first applicable rule win, including `rate_limit`. Operators had to know to low

title: chore(cdp-legacy): group batch enrichment
body:
## Problem

The `cdp-legacy-on-event` consumer enriches each event with group properties before firing webhooks.
It does this by calling `GetGroup` individually for every group type on every event in a batch -- up to 5 RPCs/queries per event, all fired concurrently via `Promise.all`.
In prod this produces many individual `GetGroup` RPCs/s in tight bursts. Same problem with the group types.

Because the personhog-router uses HTTP/2 channels behind an L4 (iptables) load balancer, all RPCs on

body:
## Problem

The rule-details "drop impact" panel previously read only \`sampling_records_dropped_by_rule\` from \`app_metrics2\` and showed *"~76 log lines dropped in the last 24 hours"*. With KB-mode rate limits now live (#59520) and bytes-by-rule accounting in app_metrics2 (#59499), line count alone doesn't tell an operator whether they're shedding 76 × 100-byte rows or 76 × 50-KB rows — exactly the question KB-mode operators need to answer to tune the limit.

title: feat(logs-ingestion): KB-based rate-limit drop rules
body:
## Problem

Drop rules of type `rate_limit` are currently configured and evaluated in **log lines per second**. A 100 logs/sec cap admits a 50-byte log just as easily as a 200 KB log, which makes it impossible to use the rule type to enforce a billing-aligned ceiling — billing measures ingested bytes, not lines. This was the original framing of the "drop rules in KB matches billing" goal.

This PR is the third and final part of a stack that closes the gap. The earlier PRs put per-row `bytes_unco

title: feat(personhog): load shed concurrent in flight requests
body:
## Problem

The personhog-replica service doesn't have any admission control. I.e. every incoming HTTP/2 stream spawns a handler future that blocks on a pool.acquire() call. Slow queries causing pool saturation will result in requests queueing unboundedly, fast queries that could complete very fast will get stuck behind hundreds of queued requests. A cascading failure. 

prod-us data:
- p50 latency degrades from 3ms to 540ms during spikes
- p99 latency reaches 1,700ms

Found that this is

title: fix(devbox): use openssh alias for devbox:ssh
body:
## Problem

`hogli devbox:ssh` connects via `coder ssh <name>`, which is Coder's standalone SSH client and does not read `~/.ssh/config`. The `IdentityAgent` and `ForwardAgent yes` that `hogli devbox:setup` writes into the `Host coder.*` block are silently ignored, so `$SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is unset inside the workspace and `git commit -S` fails:

```
error: Couldn't get agent socket?
fatal: failed to write commit object
```

Affects every engineer using Secretive or 1Password for commit signing.

`bin/deploy-hobby` rewrites `.env` on every run via `cat > .env <<EOF`, regenerating `POSTHOG_SECRET` and `ENCRYPTION_SALT_KEYS` each time. Re-running the script on a live instance — or after a partial install failure — silently invalidates all active sessions and can corrupt encrypted data.

title: feat(capture-logs): add bytes_uncompressed field for per-row byte accounting
body:
## TL;DR

Add a `bytes_uncompressed` field to the Avro log record schema to enable per-row byte accounting for drop rules. This replaces message-level accounting with row-level tracking, allowing accurate quota enforcement when Kafka messages contain thousands of log lines.

## Problem

Drop rules currently track bytes at the message level, but Kafka messages can contain thousands of individual log rows. This makes it impossible to accurately account for bytes when dropping individual rows

title: feat(hogql): use minmax skip index for materialized column range comparisons
body:
## Problem

Range comparisons (`<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`) on materialized string columns don't use the minmax skip index, even when the index is present. The printer wraps the comparison in `ifNull(...)` (nullable columns) or in `ifNull(less(nullIf(nullIf(col, ''), 'null'), x), 0)` (non-nullable columns) to handle NULL / sentinel values, and both wrappers are opaque to the ClickHouse query planner — so the minmax range probe never fires and we scan every granule.

For point queries (`=`, `IN`), the p

Cymbal's dynamic symbol-set fetch path could replace an existing uploaded symbol blob with a failed or dynamically fetched result for the same symbol reference. That made a transient parse/fetch result persistent and could block future symbolication for otherwise valid uploaded symbols.

## Changes

- Only persist fetch/parse failures when the symbol-set row has no stored blob.

body: ## Problem The notebook collab SSE has a server-side lifetime cap (similar to livestream service) — the backend closes the connection every 5 minutes (`STREAM_LIFETIME_SECONDS`) by design, so workers can be cleanly rotated. But the client doesn't reconnect after that close: `fetch-event-source` only auto-retries on errors, so users silently lose live updates from collaborators after 5 minutes. title: fix(devbox): quote IdentityAgent socket path in coder ssh config
body:
## Problem

`hogli devbox:setup` calls `coder config-ssh --ssh-option "IdentityAgent <path>"`, and that line is written verbatim into `~/.ssh/config`. If the engineer's SSH agent socket path contains whitespace — which is the default for 1Password on macOS (`/Users/<user>/Library/Group Containers/2BUA8C4S2C.com.1password/t/agent.sock`) — OpenSSH reads the trailing path components as additional keyword arguments and refuses to load the file:

```
~/.ssh/config line 62: keyword identityagent extra

title: fix(data-warehouse): surface actionable error when postgres int column type widens
body:
## Problem

A Postgres data warehouse sync was failing permanently with a raw `ArrowInvalid: Integer value 6178466636 not in range: -2147483648 to 2147483647`, with no indication of what the user should do.

Root cause: the source column's type had been widened upstream (`integer` → `bigint`) *after* the destination Delta table was created with the narrower type. The source now emits `int64` values, but the stored Delta column is still `int32`. `_evolve_pyarrow_schema` was narrowing the incoming

The treadmill has been getting bumped every few months without anyone explaining what was actually slow.

Measured across same-size shards on a recent master run (run 26214191922):

`oxlint`'s `import/no-cycle` rule flags a dependency cycle starting at `frontend/src/lib/api.ts:247` whenever the file is touched, blocking lint-staged. The cycle predates this change — the surfaced path is:

lib/api.ts -> scenes/max/maxTypes -> revenue_analytics/revenueAnalyticsLogic -> queries/schema-guards -> queries/schema/schema-general -> TaxonomicFilter/types -> entityFilterLogic -> PropertyFilters/utils -> taxonomy/helpers -> surveys/surveyQuestionLabelsLogic -> lib/api

title: feat(hogli): gate secret-wrap on per-command needs_secrets flag
body:
**Problem**

Every hogli invocation triggered the configured secret-wrap (1Password's `op run` on macOS) — including lint, format, typecheck, pre-commit hooks, and agent-spawned commands — prompting for biometric auth every time. Loud, slow, unnecessary for most commands.

**Changes**

Per-command opt-in flag `needs_secrets: true` in the manifest. Wrap only fires when the invoked subcommand has it. The framework-built-in `hogli run` is the exception (its contract is forwarding the resolved env).

body:
## Problem

Pasting a numeric-looking string (e.g. a short distinct ID like `12345`) into the Launchpad search field on the Home tab and pressing **Tab** consistently crashes the app with `TypeError: x.trim is not a function`. Any user with the AI-first homepage (Launchpad) configured is affected. Non-numeric inputs (`abc`) work fine.

## Changes

In `aiFirstHomepageLogic.ts`, coerce `searchParams.q` to a string when reading it back from the URL (in both `urlToAction` and `afterMount`).

title: feat(mcp): refactor hono mcp server
body:
The MCPServer class in the built in MCP SDK initialises a seperate MCPServer per session, which in stateless mode, means per-request.

This is very time consuming and uses ~1.1mb of memory per request, which is far too high. This removes that dependency and uses a single server that handles routing MCP requests.

A lot of this shuffles existing logic around and cleans up what we were doing previously, it adds a bunch of tests where we were lacking some.

title: fix(llma): ai column rewriter preserves select names
body:
## Problem

The `ai_events` → `events` HogQL rewriter does not preserve column names in inner queries when rewriting. Example (real queries hitting this issue are a bit more complex, but this illustrates it):
```sql
--- Original query
SELECT trace_id FROM (SELECT trace_id FROM ai_events)
--- Rewritten to
SELECT trace_id FROM (SELECT properties.$ai_trace_id FROM events)
--- Fails because the outer select now sees an `$ai_trace_id` column instead of `trace_id`
```

## Changes

```sql

Signal report artefacts are produced server-side only — the agentic Temporal pipeline writes `suggested_reviewers`, `priority_judgment`, `dismissal`, and friends, and the artefact serializer was read-only. There was no way for a client to fix a mis-resolved reviewer, drop the wrong person, or add a teammate the agent missed.

The PostHog Code coding-agent Slack mention flow ran against its own dedicated Slack app (`Integration.kind="slack-posthog-code"`), separate from the regular notifications Slack app (`kind="slack"`). This PR retires that split — the coding agent now uses the regular `kind="slack"` integration, and the

title: refactor(cdp): break events consumer inheritance with pipeline services
body:
## Problem

`CdpDatawarehouseEventsConsumer`, `CdpInternalEventsConsumer`, and `CdpPersonUpdatesConsumer` all extend `CdpEventsConsumer`. They inherit `processBatch()` which unconditionally produces hogflow invocations — even though these consumers handle event sources that aren't designed to trigger hogflows.

This inheritance causes:
- A latent bug where subclasses produce hogflow invocations they shouldn't
- Complications for the queue injection refactor (#58997), where subclasses are forced

body:
## Problem

When a user enables **Filter test accounts** on a Trends insight and then changes a series to a data warehouse table, the system correctly auto-disables the filter (DW series do not support it). But if the user then switches the insight type to Funnel, the filter re-activates — even though the user did not turn it back on, and the DW series in the same query still does not support it. The user ends up looking at the same "Reset unsupported settings" error they just got rid of.

Repro

## Problem

Funnels with `breakdown_type='session'` (e.g. on `$session_duration` or `$channel_type`) raised `ValidationError: Unsupported breakdown type: session` at query time. `BreakdownType.SESSION` is a first-class value in the schema and is fully wired through trends, but the funnels query builder was never updated to match — so any funnel insight with a session breakdown failed every render, including async paths like the Temporal `export_asset_activity` worker (subscription/dashboard imag

title: fix(replay): keep inactive periods in mp4 recording exports
body:
## Problem

The session replay MP4 export renders the recording through the recording-rasterizer, which fast-forwards through inactive segments by default. A user exporting a session as MP4 expects the full session in real time — the in-player "skip inactivity" toggle is a viewing preference, not an export contract.

## Changes

Frontend `startReplayExport` now sets `skip_inactivity: false` in the `export_context`. The backend and rasterizer already honor this flag; we just weren't sending it. T

## Problem

PostHog Code clients (Electron desktop, React Native mobile) each upload their own Expo push token to `/api/users/@me/push_tokens/`, and when a task event warrants a notification, the server fans out to every registered token. That produces phantom mobile notifications for activity the user is already watching live on desktop — and the same noise will recur with two phones, two desktops, etc.

Per-device "is the user looking at this task on another device?" can't be inferred from a s

Personhog router serializes and deserializes all request/response payloads as they flow through the service. This is unnecessary/is pure overhead because we don't need the data to make routing decisions.

Adds RawProxyService, a `tower` service that operates below the gRPC layer on just raw HTTP/2 frames

I was wondering how the behavior was going to be with multiple readers getting a miss at the same time. This PR is just documenting this herd problem here, as I am testing this more to understand the implications of a lazy computation on the read path vs an eager ensure computation like cache warming.

## Problem

A customer reported that clicking **Save** in the survey editor did nothing — no API request, no error, no toast. The survey was an API-distributed rating survey with `display: emoji` and `scale: 2` (a thumbs-up/down question) where `lowerBoundLabel` and `upperBoundLabel` were empty strings.

For thumb questions the editor hides the lower/upper bound-label inputs (`SurveyEditQuestionRow.tsx`, `!isThumbQuestion(question)` gate), but the kea-forms validator still required them. kea-for

title: fix(remote-config): dual-write hypercache to shared redis
body:
## Problem

`RemoteConfig.get_hypercache()` writes to the dedicated flags Redis (via `FLAGS_DEDICATED_CACHE_ALIAS`), but the hypercache-server that serves `/array/<token>/config[.js]` reads from the shared Redis (its own `REDIS_URL` env var). In production these point at different ElastiCache clusters, so Django's writes never reach the Redis the hypercache-server reads. The hypercache-server then falls through to S3 — which can hold stale blobs written before the dedicated-cache wiring landed.

title: feat(hogli): add devbox:setup --reset-* + Y/n gate

## Problem

`hogli devbox:setup` had no way to undo a setting. The dotfiles prompt prefilled the saved URL as the default, so pressing Enter just re-saved it; even if you submitted blank, the config helper had no `clear_*` counterpart and existing workspaces kept the baked-in `dotfiles_uri` parameter — meaning a once-saved repo would keep re-cloning its `.zshrc` on every boot until you wiped it via the Coder UI. Re-runs also printed two lines of "Using saved X / Run --configure-X to change" pe

> Rule semgrep.rules.X contains an [include/exclude] pattern 'foo/bar.py' that will soon be interpreted as '/foo/bar.py' to comply with the Semgrepignore v2 and Gitignore specifications.

Semgrep is migrating to Gitignore-style anchoring: bare paths in `paths.include` / `paths.exclude` will soon be treated

title: feat(tasks): add server-side archived flag to tasks
body:
## Problem

PostHog Code's desktop (Electron) and mobile (React Native) clients each maintain task archive state in their own local store — desktop in SQLite (`ArchiveRepository`), mobile in AsyncStorage (`useArchivedTasksStore`). Archiving a task on mobile leaves it visible on desktop and vice versa, because the server never sees the flag.

Tasks are project entities served from `/api/projects/{team_id}/tasks/`. Promoting archive state to the Task model lets both clients sync through the existi

title: fix(flags): reject leading-zero versions in sortableSemVer
body:
## Problem

The HogQL `sortableSemVer` function (used to compute the rollout
**blast-radius preview** for feature flags with semver comparisons)
extracted the first dot-separated digit run from the property value
without enforcing strict semver rules. As a result `\"3.07\"` was
parsed as `[3, 7]` — exactly the same as `\"3.7\"` — so the preview
counted users with version `\"3.07\"` as matching a `version >= 3.7`
filter.

Runtime flag evaluation (Rust, [`semver`](https://docs.rs/semver) crate)

title: feat(experiments): anchor retention maturity on start_event timestamp

## Problem

The maturity filter for retention metrics was anchored on exposure timestamp, not start_event timestamp. That means a user exposed long ago who only did the start_event recently would pass the maturity check, even though their retention window has barely opened. They'd usually get counted as "not retained" and dilute the metric.

## Changes

- Retention's maturity HAVING now lives inside the `start_events` CTE, anchored on the user's start_event timestamp (`min` or `max` of tim

title: feat(flags): block bots
body:
## Problem

Googlebot crawls embedded posthog-js pages and the SDK fires POST /flags for every page load. The Rust feature-flags service runs the full pipeline (auth, billing, flag evaluation, hash key overrides) for traffic that has no human behind it, burning quota and creating phantom users. posthog-js gates `capture()` on `_is_bot()` at `posthog-core.ts:1217` but never applies the same check to `_callFlagsEndpoint()`. The affected customer also fronts their site with CloudFront, which rewr

title: fix(analytics-platform): disable INSERT quorum in local dev so lazy precompute does not hang
body:
## Problem

Local dev runs against a single-replica ClickHouse, but the lazy computation framework hardcodes `insert_quorum: \"auto\"` on every INSERT. On a single-node setup, ClickHouse writes immediately but still blocks on the quorum protocol — the INSERT then hits the 600s `max_execution_time` and dies with:

```
Code: 319. Unknown quorum status. The data was inserted in the local replica
  but we could not verify quorum. Reason: Timeout while waiting for quorum:
  Insertion status: Wrote 1

## Problem

The lazy computation framework only emits a structured `lazy_computation.executed` log line per invocation. Useful for following an individual request, but to answer "what's the cache hit ratio on the lazy precompute path" you need log-based aggregation (Loki/Grafana) rather than a first-class Prometheus time series.

The framework had no Counter for total invocations, hits, or misses — only the `web_overview_lazy_precompute_failed_total` Counter scoped to web_overview failures.

## Problem

The billing settings page lets any customer self-serve subscribe to the **Enterprise** platform add-on at \$2,000/month with one click. Enterprise pricing should be a sales-led conversation — the self-serve path skips contract terms, SCIM/SSO rollout discussion, dedicated account management, and the rest of the things that make Enterprise actually Enterprise.

Users searching for the logout option were typing logout-related queries ("logout", "sign out", etc.) into Project/Account Settings and the command palette, getting no results, and asking Max — which gave incorrect Settings-based guidance because it had no canonical pointer to where logout lives. ## Problem

The PostHog MCP v2 cannot read dashboard layout because the table that stores it (`posthog_dashboardtile`, the `DashboardTile` join model) is not exposed in HogQL's `system.*` schema. HogQL's ClickHouse printer requires every `PostgresTable` to have a `team_id` column — it auto-injects `WHERE team_id = <ctx.team_id>` against every query. `DashboardTile` reaches its team only through `dashboard.team_id`, so the table cannot be registered.

We’re doing a lot of work on every request, lets try frontload as much work as possible so we can make initialisation faster.

This brings init time down from ~400ms to ~15ms in local testing, and reduced how much memory is used initialising.

title: fix(data-warehouse): use cursor pagination for zendesk tickets import
body:
## Problem

The Zendesk tickets import uses the **time-based** incremental export (`/api/v2/incremental/tickets`). This endpoint has a documented failure mode: when more than one page of tickets share the same `generated_timestamp` (common when a bulk update or migration stamps thousands of older tickets at once), the cursor can never advance past that timestamp. Pagination then loops forever re-fetching the same boundary page.

The downstream effects compound:

- The sync never reaches `end_of_

title: chore(logs): move capture-logs management port off 8081

## Problem

The `capture-logs` Rust service started by `bin/start-rust-service` exports `MANAGEMENT_BIND_PORT=8081`, which collides with the local `temporal-ui` container. When both run together, the temporal UI is unreachable on `localhost:8081` (or the management endpoint fails to bind, depending on start order).

The 8081 binding landed alongside an unrelated larger change and was never intended to be sticky — it can move freely.

## Changes

- `bin/start-rust-service`: set `MANAGEMENT_BIND_P

`tools/hogli/` is the **generic** dev-CLI framework published on PyPI as `hogli`. It is supposed to be reusable by any project that wants a YAML-driven dev CLI.

But two recent PRs baked PostHog-specifics into the framework:

- #45696 hardcoded the filenames `.env.development` / `.env.services` / `.env.local` and the `op://` substring into `hogli/cli.py`, and added a `hogli run` subcommand that knows about 1Password's CLI.

Customer feedback flagged that when editing a SQL insight, there is no way to rename it or update its description from the SQL editor view. The only workaround is to click "Update insight" to save SQL changes, navigate back to the regular insight view, and then use the pencil icons there. title: fix(phrocs): use truecolor selection bg in copy mode
body:
## Problem

In phrocs's copy mode (anchor + cursor selection), the in-between lines were highlighted with `lipgloss.NewStyle().Background(colorBlack)` — i.e. ANSI 0. Zed's terminal maps ANSI 0 to the same value as the default terminal background, so the highlight painted invisibly and users couldn't tell that the mouse/keyboard was extending the selection rather than just moving the cursor. Ghostty, VS Code's terminal, and Cursor's terminal all map ANSI 0 to a distinct color, so the bug only sho

title: feat(phrocs): make `r` restart crashed procs too
body:
## Problem

In phrocs the `r` hotkey only worked on running procs — if a proc had crashed, `r` did nothing and you had to reach for `s` (start) instead. Two different keys for the same user intent ("rerun this thing") is friction, especially because the proc-detail view doesn't make it obvious which key applies to which state.

## Changes

- `r` now also starts a proc in `StatusCrashed`, alongside its existing behavior of restarting a running proc. Clean exits (`StatusDone`), manual stops (`Stat

body:
## Problem

A User paths insight that uses both a Starts at and Ends at URL silently returns "There are no matching events" when either URL ends in a trailing slash — even when the same start/end pages clearly converted in a funnel insight over the same date range.

This is because `construct_event_hogql` already normalizes captured URLs via `replaceRegexpAll(..., '(.)/$', '\1')`, so `compact_path` only contains slash-trimmed URLs. But `get_target_clause` was interpolating the raw filter values

This indirection makes it easy to misconfigure.

title: feat(max): add hands-free voice mode for mobile chat

## Problem

This PR adds a hands-free mode so a user can talk to Max with their hands and eyes off the screen.

The use case is mobile web specifically — gym/walking. Desktop visibility is for familiarity, not the primary case.

title: chore: forbid new wildcard imports via ruff F403

## Problem

The codebase had 84 `from X import *` statements across 28 files. Wildcard imports
hide a module's public surface, make refactors risky (transitive name leaks can
break callers far away), and slow down static analysis. There was no rule to stop
new ones from creeping in — `F403` was in the global ruff `ignore` list with a
stale comment claiming "~67" occurrences.

## Changes

- **Enforce going forward:** removed `F403` from `[tool.ruff.lint] ignore` so any
  new `from X import *` fai

body:
## Problem

Users running background agents in sandboxes are hitting 429s from the LLM gateway — roughly ~30% of background-agent runs fail today. The current per-user cap for the `background_agents` product is `$100 / 24h` (burst) and `$1000 / 30d` (sustained), and the daily burst window is the one being exhausted in practice. We want to reduce the 429 rate without raising the overall spend ceiling for a single user.

## Changes

In `services/llm-gateway/src/llm_gateway/config.py`, update the `

title: fix(llma): correct gemini-3-pro-image cost undercounted 10x

2. The exact lookup against `google-ai-studio` misses — the cost record for that model only ships `default`, `google-ai-studio-global`, and `google-vertex-global` keys.

title: fix(slack): keep thread root when collecting context for @PostHog tasks
body:
## Problem

When a user replies to a PostHog Slack alert with \`@PostHog lets investigate this\`, the task that gets created should pick up the alert message as its context. In practice the task description was just the user's reply text, so the agent had to ask "what should I look at?" — losing all the alert detail (insight, breach values, dates).

The cause is in [\`_collect_thread_messages\`](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/blob/master/products/slack_app/backend/api.py#L651): it filters ev

title: fix(dev): acquire bin/start flock after 1Password re-exec
body:
## Problem

When `.env.local` contains 1Password `op://` refs, `bin/start` re-execs itself under `op run` to resolve them. The current ordering acquires the flock on `fd 9` **before** that re-exec, so the inherited `fd 9` lives on inside the `op run` process and keeps the lock held. The re-exec'd child `bin/start` then can't acquire the same lock and aborts with:

> ⚠️ Another instance of bin/start is already running (lock file: ...)

…even when nothing else is running. The only workaround was t

body:
## Problem

The anomaly investigation agent appends a "See /notebooks/<short_id> for the full investigation." line to the breach descriptions that flow into the firing Slack message and the email notification. Because the URL is relative, Slack and email clients render it as plain text, so users can't jump straight to the notebook from the alert.

## Changes

`posthog/temporal/ai/anomaly_investigation/workflow.py` — build the notebook URL via `absolute_uri()` so it resolves against `SITE_URL` (e

title: perf(cymbal): reduce memory pressure and unblock tokio workers in symbol store
body:
## Problem

The cymbal HTTP service OOMs under load and frequently times out (>30s) when processing large sourcemaps or dSYMs. Several issues in the symbol-store path were amplifying peak memory and tail latency: S3 reads were buffered then re-copied, large blobs were cloned again at parse time, CPU-bound parsing ran on the tokio runtime, and the per-batch issue cache was rebuilt on every request.

## Changes

- Threaded `bytes::Bytes` through `BlobClient` and all four symbol-set parsers so blob

## Changes

- New `default_namespace` field on each pool entry. `toolbox-django` → `posthog-toolbox-django`, `flags-cache-jumphost` → `posthog` (unchanged in practice).
- `main()` reads the namespace as `os.environ.get

Roughly 20% context-mill requests to github are throttled. This will do a single request in 10 minutes.

After these changes, the flow will be:
- Revalidate on warmup, serve stale while revalidating.
- Revalidate in init, serve stale while revalidating.
- Only one parallel lock is possible.
- If all fail for some reason, serve from the network.

## Problem

A user on the onboarding `invite_teammates` step repeatedly clicked the Continue button without it responding, and JS exceptions fired in the same session. Root cause: `OnboardingInviteTeammates.tsx` wired the continue handler as a silent `&&` chain (`email_service_available && target_email && canSubmit && inviteTeamMembers()`). When any condition was falsy — invalid email, owner-level confirmation not typed, missing fields — the action no-op'd and the button gave no feedback. The us

title: fix(health-menu): handle incident.io status fetch failures
body:
## Problem

The globally-mounted HealthMenu polls `posthogstatus.com/api/v1/summary` every 5 minutes (and on visibility change) to surface PostHog status. The fetch had no error handling — every network failure surfaced as an unhandled `TypeError: Failed to fetch` and got reported to error tracking.

Because the call hits an external domain, failures are common and outside our control: ad blockers, tracking-protection extensions, DNS hiccups, transient status-page outages, or a backgrounded tab

The "Add revenue event" entry-point button at the top of the Revenue analytics settings page does nothing when clicked. Users trying to self-serve a `payment_success`/`revenue`/static-USD event get stuck and contact support instead of completing setup.

Root cause: the outer button piped a DOM ref (`eventsButtonRef`) through `AccessControlAction` to the inner `LemonButton`, then called `eventsButtonRef.current?.click()` on click. But `AccessControlAction` is a `forwardRef` that re-cl

title: fix(oauth): return 400 invalid_grant on OAuthAccessToken.DoesNotExist

## Problem

`/oauth/token` has been returning 500s in recurring, escalating spikes (signal fingerprint `b6d469a5...`, fired seven times between 2026-05-06 and 2026-05-13). `OAuthTokenView.post` calls `super().post()` at `posthog/api/oauth/views.py:689`, which delegates to django-oauth-toolkit. That code path performs an access-token re-read that can raise `OAuthAccessToken.DoesNotExist` — typically on double-redeemed authorization codes or concurrent token requests for the same code. The wrapper

title: fix(sdk-doctor): return 200 for teams with no SDK data

## Problem

The legacy `GET /api/sdk_doctor/` endpoint returned HTTP 500 for any team without cached SDK versions when not running in `DEBUG` mode. Brand-new teams have no Redis cache entry and no ClickHouse events, so `get_team_data` legitimately returns `{}` — but the caller's `if not team_data:` branch collapsed that empty-but-valid case with the genuine-failure case, falling through to `Response({"error": ...}, status=500)`.

Pasting raw markdown into a notebook drops the source in as a single plain-text run instead of rendering it. Anyone bringing in pre-written docs or analysis notes has to hand-convert every heading and list with slash commands. Notion and Linear both detect markdown on paste; this PR brings notebooks in line.

Closes #49740

title: feat(propvals): part 1: add property_count col to kafka_property_values
body:
## Problem

The Part 2 aggregator emits Kafka messages with an accumulated `property_count` per `(team_id, type, key, value)` tuple per flush window, and commits the produce + the consumer offsets atomically through a Kafka transaction. ClickHouse's `kafka_property_values` table needs a column to receive that count, and the MV that feeds the storage table needs to read from the new column instead of hardcoding `toUInt64(1)`.

[Data flow diagram](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/issues/5230

title: feat(user-interviews): move api+webhooks into presentation/, enforce isolation
body:
## Problem

After #59132 and #59133, the only remaining cross-product imports of `user_interviews` internals are:

- `posthog/api/__init__.py` importing the three viewsets from `backend/api.py`
- `posthog/urls.py` importing the Vapi webhook handlers from `backend/webhooks.py`

Both are presentation concerns. Moving them under `backend/presentation/` lets us tighten the `tach` boundary so the product is treated as fully isolated, which unlocks `backend:contract-check` and selective testing via tu

title: feat(user-interviews): migrate cross-product callers to facade
body:
## Problem

`posthog/api/sharing.py` and `posthog/settings/web.py` both reach into `products/user_interviews/backend/api.py` directly — a private helper (`_parse_identifier`) and a module-level constant (`SEARCH_DOCUMENT_TYPES`). With the facade introduced in #59132 in place, those two callers can now go through the public surface instead.

## Changes

- `posthog/api/sharing.py` uses `UserInterviewsAPI.parse_interviewee_identifier(...).display_name` instead of the private `_parse_identifier` tup

title: feat(user-interviews): add facade + contracts for product isolation
body:
## Problem

The `user_interviews` product currently exposes its internals (`models`, viewsets, helpers) directly to other parts of the codebase. That means CI can't safely treat it as an isolated package — any change to its test files re-runs the full Django suite, and any other change to core (`posthog/`, `ee/`) that touches a file using those internals re-runs the user_interviews tests too. See [products/architecture.md](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/blob/master/products/architecture.md)

body:
## Problem

On the LLM analytics Sentiment tab, many cards render blank — the
sentiment bar, percentage, and "View trace" link are all there, but the
user message text is empty. The matching trace view shows the message
correctly, so it isn't a data ingestion problem.

Root cause: `_SENTIMENT_GENERATIONS_HEAVY_SQL` has no `LIMIT`, so
`execute_hogql_query` (via `LimitContext.QUERY`) injects its default of
100 rows. Whenever the preflight returns more than 100 trace_ids (it
caps at `GENERATIONS_QU

title: feat(oauth): restrict and revoke tokens minted during impersonation sessions
body:
OAuth tokens minted during staff impersonation sessions are now restricted to read-only scopes and automatically revoked when impersonation ends.

**What changed:**

When a staff user is impersonating a customer and an OAuth flow is initiated, the following protections are applied:

- **Scope downgrade:** Any write scopes (including the `*` wildcard) requested during an active read-only impersonation session are silently converted to their `<object>:read` equivalents before the token is issued.

title: feat(login): add dev-only one-click login UI
body:
Adds a "Dev login" section to the login page that is only visible when running in debug mode. This allows developers to instantly sign in as any active user without entering a password, eliminating the friction of looking up credentials during local development.

A new `DevLoginViewSet` is registered at `api/login/dev` that returns 404 in non-debug environments, ensuring the endpoint never exists in production. The `list` action returns up to 50 active users, and the `create` action authenticate

## Problem

The `Storybook` workflow on `master` has been failing about half of all completed
runs, blocking the required `Visual regression tests pass` check on the default

Review run is created with `--purpose observe` (tracking-only, non-gating).

1. **(P1)** `models-metrics.md` "projection-friendly" mean query used `sum(value) / sum(count)` — but `count` is a per-row `UInt64` column (histogram observation count), not the aggregate `count()`. An agent following the prompt could write `SUM(value)` over a window on a cumulative counter and get wildly wrong totals (cumulative values are running totals — summing them double-counts).

Follow-up to #59094, which converted `cyclotron_jobs.person_id` from `UUID` to `TEXT`. The adapter labelled each ad with `ad_group_ad.ad.name`, but Google only populates that field for a handful of legacy display ad types. For the search, responsive and image ads that make up modern accounts it is always empty — so the column rendered with no label at all.

title: feat(workflows): Switch cyclotron_jobs.person_id from UUID to TEXT
body:
## Problem

`cyclotron_jobs.person_id` is typed as `UUID`, but person identifiers can actually take non-UUID shapes - e.g. UUIDT-style ids with non-standard version bits - that Postgres's `UUID` type rejects. Today the producers coerce non-UUID values to null so inserts don't fail, which means the matcher (and any other consumer) can never wake parked jobs keyed by those identifiers.

The wait-until-event feature is not yet live, so we can drop existing data and re-create the column as TEXT with

The `querying-posthog-data` skill is the canonical HogQL reference for agents using `execute-sql`. Its `references/` directory documents every entity domain except the OpenTelemetry data-plane tables (`logs`, `trace_spans`, `metrics`). Agents have no schema reference for cross-signal joins, exemplar lookups, or aggregations that don't fit the typed `query-*` tools.

The `execute-sql` MCP prompt template instructs agents to use HogQL for queries that fall outside the typed `query-*` tools, but does not document the three OpenTelemetry data-plane tables agents can actually reach: `logs`, `trace_spans`, and `metrics` (the latter newly available after [#50936](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/50936)). Without this surfacing, agents either can't find the tables at all or guess at column names.

This is part of a multi-PR Phase 1 effort to make

`$survey_response_<question-id>` is the per-question property that PostHog captures for every survey response. title: chore(typescript): add tsgo (TS 7) as a shadow type-checker

## Problem

`tsc --noEmit` is the slowest gate on most TypeScript PRs. The frontend job already runs with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16384` and can take a minute or more. TypeScript 7 (the Go port, `tsgo`) is now in beta and offers a ~5-10× speed-up. The programmatic Compiler API for TS 7 is explicitly _not ready_ until at least TS 7.1, so tools like kea-typegen, ts-jest, ts-node, parcel, and ts-json-schema-generator can't move yet — they still need `typescript@6` resolvable in the dep

title: fix(batch-exports): cancel hanging Databricks queries on timeout/cancellation
body:
## Problem

Databricks batch export activities could hang for a long time. There are two main issues we're addressing:

1. **No client-side timeout on `execute_query`.** `cursor.execute` ran in `asyncio.to_thread` with no `asyncio.timeout` around it — if the warehouse went unresponsive, the worker thread just sat there.
2. **No server-side cancel on cancellation.** Propagating `CancelledError` did nothing for the underlying thread, because `asyncio.to_thread` cannot cancel it. The Databricks wor

title: fix(support): prevent duplicate Zendesk tickets on rapid Submit clicks

## Problem

Users submitting support tickets via the in-app side panel (and the legacy modal) could occasionally end up with **two Zendesk tickets** for a single submission. The trigger was a fast double-click — or a slow connection that gave the user time for a follow-up click before the network call returned.

The form's kea-forms `submit` dispatched `actions.submitZendeskTicket(formValues)` and returned immediately (fire-and-forget). Because `submit` didn't `await`, the auto-generated `isSend

title: fix(logs): exclude resources matching any negative attribute filter
body:
## Problem

`_generate_resource_attribute_filters` in `products/logs/backend/logs_query_runner.py` builds a single subquery for all negative resource attribute filters. The filters get inverted (`!=` → `=`) and combined with `arrayAll` in the `HAVING` clause, then excluded with `NOT IN`.

With one negative filter this works. With two or more, `arrayAll` requires every inverted condition to match, so a resource is only excluded when it matches *all* of them. The intent is to exclude a resource if

title: fix(data-warehouse): use inclusive cursor on date-typed incremental sync
body:
## Problem

SQL data warehouse sources (Postgres, Redshift, MySQL, MSSQL, BigQuery, Snowflake) advance their incremental cursor with `WHERE incremental_field > saved_cursor`. For DateTime / Timestamp / Integer / Numeric / ObjectID columns the cursor carries enough resolution that `>` is safe — two distinct rows essentially never share the boundary value.

For `IncrementalFieldType.Date`, the cursor only carries day granularity. If sync A completes mid-day and saves cursor `2026-05-13`, sync B's

broken for Opus 4.7. This surfaced as an LLM Gateway 5xx error-rate alert when upstream
Anthropic was returning `529 Overloaded` and the fallback could not absorb traffic.

Update `ANTHROPIC_TO_BEDROCK_MODEL_MAP` for `claude-opus

title: fix(error-tracking): wrap argMax in tuple to keep NULL unassignments
body:
## Problem

Big thanks to @cat-ph - he discovered that.

`argMax(field, version)` skips NULLs in `field`. When a user unassigns an issue, the new version row has `assigned_user_id = NULL`, but the query falls back to the previous non-NULL value — UI keeps showing the old assignee. Same issue for any nullable field that legitimately transitions to NULL.

## Changes

Wrap nullable fields in `tupleElement(argMax(tuple(x), version), 1)` so NULLs survive the aggregation.

## How did you tes

The feature flags service shows a sustained 5xx spike driven entirely by `cookieless_error` / `SaltCacheError::DateOutOfRange`, almost all of it from a bot crawling a specific team. title: fix(hogql): restore countDistinct aggregation registration

## Problem

Root cause: commit 08af413 (#41515, "combinate all the aggregations") moved `count`, `sum`, `min`, `max`, `avg`, `countDistinct`, and `median` into `COMBINATOR_AGGREGATIONS` so suffix-decorated variants like `countDistinctIf`, `co

On the person view, the MRR and Lifetime value rows in the info box could render the literal string `unknown` even when revenue analytics was fully enabled and configured for the team. The "Require completed conversion window" toggle does nothing for retention metrics. The feature only checks the `conversion_window` field, but retention metrics store their time window in `retention_window`. So when you run a day-7 retention experiment, the denominator gets diluted by everyone who hasn't been around long enough to reach day 7.

## Changes

title: fix: Imprve personless distinct id query performance
body:
## Problem

`delete_bulky_postgres_data` deletes `posthog_personlessdistinctid` rows in batches via the shared `_raw_delete_batch` helper, which does two roundtrips per batch (a `values_list` SELECT then a composite-tuple `DELETE … WHERE ("team_id", "id") IN ((…))`). On large teams this is slow: the planner picks a sequential or bitmap scan, and the outer DELETE pays a per-row primary-key lookup on top of the index maintenance work. Measured throughput on a real team was ~400 rows/sec.

## C

title: fix(replay): cap shared-link snapshot fetches per token, not per IP
body:
## Problem

Replay endpoints reached via a sharing-token authenticator get rate-limited at free-tier rates per IP, regardless of the team's plan.

- `_TierAwareReplayThrottle` only applies tier rates for personal-API-key requests; sharing-token requests stay on `SNAPSHOT_RATES["free"]` (12/min, 60/hour).
- The cache key falls through to the client IP, so corporate-NAT and embed viewers collide. A single recording's player fires one request per block, so any session with more than ~12 blocks 429s

Those went through the \`else\` branch and reached \`strftime\` unchanged, still rendering the UTC day.

## Changes

Generalized the tz re-anchoring (\`_to_team_tz

Running `hogli start` on a fresh checkout fails the MCP server process with:

✘ [ERROR] The directory specified by the "assets.directory" field in your configuration file does not exist:

`services/mcp/wrangler.jsonc` declares `assets.directory: "./public/"`, but the directory only exists once `pnpm build:ui-apps` has run (it writes into `public/ui-apps/`, which is gitignored). Because `public/` itself has no tracked files, a clean clone has no `public

See https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C076E99B152/p1779193894977349

## Problem

HogQL/Hog source is parsed by an ANTLR-generated C++ parser (`cpp-json`) and a Python `ParseTreeVisitor` fallback (`python`). This adds a third, hand-rolled Rust parser as the `rust-json` backend, a step toward a faster, memory-safe parser whose output can be checked against the C++ reference before it ever carries production traffic.

After #58177 shipped, the new `stats_table_*_query` tags surfaced the strategy breakdown for the stats table runner, but external clicks queries kept emitting the legacy `stats_table_query` tag — making it look (in `system.query_log` and the cache warmer) like

title: fix(replay): skip too-fresh recordings in kiosk mode
body:
## Problem

Customer report: kiosk gets stuck on the "We're still working on it" hedgehog screen. Root cause: kiosk orders recordings by `start_time DESC` and the freshest recordings are the ones most likely to still be ingesting — the player shows that empty state when `isRecentAndInvalid` (`snapshots invalid && start < 5 min ago`). The 5-min stuck-timeout eventually advances kiosk to the next recording, which on a busy site is also too fresh, so the loop never finds a playable one.

## Changes

The hosted survey editor has three columns — flow rail, form editor, preview. The form editor and preview duplicate effort: you type in column two and watch the same text render in column three. For a product that's one question per screen, the canvas should be the editor.

title: chore(logs): add manual refresh button for drop rule 24h impact
body:
## Problem

A drop rule's detail page shows `~N log lines dropped in the last 24 hours (ingestion)`, sourced from `app_metrics2` in ClickHouse. The ingestion worker writes those rows in periodic batches, so the number lags real-time drops by ~30–60 seconds. While iterating on a rule, users had no way to retry the query short of refreshing the whole page, and there was no surfaced hint that the lag was a known property of the metric pipeline.

## Changes

- Add a small `IconRefresh` button

title: feat(logs): evaluate filter_group in ingestion drop rules
body:
## Problem

The drop-rules UI from #58898 writes a `config.filter_group` (universal property filter group — same primitive the logs viewer and alerts use), but the ingestion worker still only honoured the legacy `config.patterns` regex list. Rules created through the new UI were no-ops on the drop path — they appeared in the rule list, the form saved them successfully, but no log lines were actually dropped.

This PR makes them actually drop.

## Changes

Wires per-record evaluation of `

title: feat(surveys): add hosted-only templates and mode-aware filtering
body:
## Problem

Hosted and in-app surveys are different products in a meaningful way. In-app templates lean on SDK triggers (page events, exceptions, onboarding completion) and short cognitive load (one question in a popover). Hosted surveys are accessed by clicking a link, so they tolerate longer flows and need different framing — a recruitment intake, a long-form research survey, a post-call NPS.

Today the wizard's template grid shows the same templates regardless of mode. Picking "Announcement"
